functional.description: Collect host resources metrics (CPU %, Memory %, Disk %, Network I/O).
Monitor database health (connection count, query latency).
Store system metrics in the database every 10 seconds.
Display system metrics with interactive graphs in the dashboard.
Configure alerts for critical resource thresholds (CPU > 80%, Memory > 85%, Disk > 90%).

nonFunctional.description: Metric collection should not fail if external monitoring services (Prometheus) are down.
Minimal impact on system resources (~1-2% CPU overhead).

constraints.description: Use Prometheus or StatsD client for collection.

assumptions.description: The executing environment allows access to OS-level resource information.

domainModel.description: Record of host and infrastructure health at a point in time.

interactions.description: Cron/Timer triggers -> Gather OS data -> Gather DB data -> Write to Local DB -> Export to Prometheus.

edgeCases.handling: Log error to console and suppress further writes to avoid cascade failure.

terminology.definition: A popular open-source monitoring and alerting toolkit.
